fact: November 1 is exactly 305 days into the year, which means there are only 60 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, this is when squirrels go absolutely nuts burying acorns - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun now sets before 6 PM in most northern places, making it feel like bedtime when you're still doing homework.
November 1 marks the start of the only month that doesn't share its first letter with any other month - November is totally unique!
This is prime time for seeing the Northern Lights in northern regions because the nights are getting longer and darker, perfect for spotting those dancing green curtains in the sky.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, November 1 is when jacaranda trees typically explode with purple flowers so bright they look fake!
In northern climates, deer are usually in their mating season right now, which means male deer are running around with giant antlers acting totally crazy and making weird honking sounds.
The temperature difference between day and night often gets super dramatic around this time in temperate regions - you might need shorts at lunch and a winter coat for dinner!
November 1 is exactly halfway between the autumn equinox and winter solstice, making it the perfect middle point of fall.
This is when spiders in cold places typically start sneaking into houses like tiny eight-legged refugees looking for warmth.
Maple trees are dropping their leaves so fast right now that a single tree can dump 200,000 leaves in just a few weeks!
The air pressure changes around this time make some people's joints ache, but it also makes soap bubbles last longer and fly farther than in summer.
